text: ‘Whereas the rich man takes pride in what in truth abases him’; others would interpret, ‘Let the rich (Christian) take pride in his abasement’, that is, in bearing reproach for the sake of his religion; but this does not seem to fit the context so well.

‘God may threaten us with evil’; this seems to be the only possible meaning of the Latin, if the text here is correct. The sense of the Greek is, ‘God is unversed in evil’, or perhaps ‘is not tempted by evil’; others would render it, ‘does not tempt men to evil’.
‘The face he was born with’; some understand this as meaning ‘the face which belongs to his perishable body’.
